steps.title: Find the obtuse angle measure using supplementary angles
steps.content:
When a transversal intersects any line, adjacent angles are supplementary (sum to 180°). Since one acute angle is $(9x - 490)°$, the adjacent obtuse angle is:

$$\text{Obtuse angle} = 180 - (9x - 490) = 670 - 9x \text{ degrees}$$

By the properties of parallel lines (corresponding and alternate angles), all 4 acute angles equal $(9x - 490)°$ and all 4 obtuse angles equal $670 - 9x$ degrees.

steps.title: Set up and simplify the sum equation
steps.content:
Calculate one acute angle plus three obtuse angles:

$$\text{Sum} = (9x - 490) + 3(670 - 9x)$$

$$= 9x - 490 + 2010 - 27x$$

$$= -18x + 1520$$

Since this sum equals $(-18x + w)°$, we have $w = 1520$.
